title: Dorothea's Journey
description: From idealistic Miss Brooke to her disastrous marriage with Casaubon, through disillusionment and friendship with Lydgate, to choosing love with Will Ladislaw over wealth and respectability.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea encourage Mr. Casaubon's visits and show eager interest in his scholarly work?
decisions.context: Dorothea is captivated by Casaubon's learning. She envisions helping with his great intellectual project. Sir James Chettam also shows interest, but she finds him intellectually limited.

yes.text: Yes - encourage his visits and conversations
yes.consequence: Her fascination grows. She sees herself as a future helpmate to genius.

no.consequence: AI generates alternative path where she focuses on her cottage-building plans instead.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea reject Sir James Chettam's suit?
decisions.context: Celia reveals that Sir James is courting Dorothea. He's kind, wealthy, and supports her cottage plans. But Dorothea finds him dull. She wants intellectual companionship.

yes.consequence: She makes clear she could never marry him. Sir James is heartbroken but will later marry Celia (good match for both).

no.consequence: AI generates path where she reconsiders his practical virtues.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea accept Mr. Casaubon's marriage proposal?
decisions.context: Casaubon has proposed. Dorothea is joyful - this is her chance to be useful, to hold the lamp to greatness. She doesn't see his pedantry, coldness, or that his 'great work' is delusional. ERROR #1.

yes.consequence: She accepts with joy despite age difference and Mr. Brooke's mild concerns. The trap closes.

no.consequence: AI generates alternative path where she avoids this disastrous marriage.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea suppress her growing disillusionment with Casaubon during the honeymoon?
decisions.context: In Rome, viewing the reclining Ariadne statue (abandoned by Theseus), Dorothea recognizes her own abandonment. Casaubon is pedantic, cold - his work is futile. She weeps alone. ERROR #2: She doesn't voice her disappointment.

yes.text: Yes - suppress her feelings and try to be dutiful
yes.consequence: She buries her disappointment, trying harder to be helpful. The marriage becomes a prison.

no.text: No - confess her disappointment to Casaubon
no.consequence: AI generates path where honest communication might change their dynamic.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea confide her marital unhappiness to Will Ladislaw when they meet in Rome?
decisions.context: Will Ladislaw appears in Rome - youthful, artistic, alive. He sees her unhappiness. She's drawn to his vitality but loyal to Casaubon.

yes.text: Yes - confide in Will about her disappointment
yes.consequence: AI generates path where early honesty about her feelings changes their relationship.

no.consequence: She keeps her unhappiness private, though Will perceives it. Their connection grows silently.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate (attending the dying Casaubon) recognize Dorothea's devotion to an unworthy husband?
decisions.context: Lydgate sees Dorothea's selfless care for Casaubon. He's moved by her devotion to a man who doesn't deserve it. This plants the seed of his admiration and friendship. HOPE begins for possible Dorothea-Lydgate pairing.

yes.text: Yes - he sees and admires her devotion
yes.consequence: Lydgate begins to respect and care for Dorothea deeply. Their friendship bond begins.

no.consequence: AI generates path where their friendship never develops.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea remain unconscious of her growing attraction to Will Ladislaw?
decisions.context: Will visits more frequently. Dorothea feels alive in his presence. Casaubon grows jealous and suspicious. ERROR #3: Dorothea hasn't admitted to herself what she feels. Even Casaubon sees it before she does.

yes.text: Yes - remain unconscious of her true feelings
yes.consequence: She enjoys Will's company as 'innocent friendship' while Casaubon seethes with suspicion.

no.consequence: AI generates path where conscious recognition changes her choices.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea go to the garden to consider Casaubon's request that she promise to complete his work after his death?
decisions.context: Casaubon asks her to promise she'll carry out his wishes - complete the Key to All Mythologies. She hesitates, needs time to think. She WOULD have promised (ERROR #4 narrowly avoided).

yes.text: Yes - go to the garden to think
yes.consequence: She wrestles with duty vs. self-preservation. She finds Casaubon unconscious in the garden.

no.consequence: AI generates path where she promises instantly and ruins her future.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate prevent Dorothea from seeing Casaubon to give her answer about the promise?
decisions.context: Casaubon is dying in bed. Dorothea has decided to promise YES. Lydgate keeps her from entering the room. Is this deliberate? Does he know what promise she wants to make? He SAVES HER LIFE by this obstruction. CRUCIAL friendship moment.

yes.text: Yes - keep her from the room until Casaubon dies
yes.consequence: Lydgate (deliberately?) prevents her from promising away her life. Casaubon dies before she can answer. She is saved. Their friendship deepens profoundly.

no.text: No - let her enter to make the promise
no.consequence: AI generates path where she promises and is trapped forever in Casaubon's dead wishes.

decisions.decision: Will the discovery of Casaubon's codicil (forbidding her from marrying Will or losing her inheritance) make Dorothea realize her own feelings?
decisions.context: The codicil reveals Casaubon suspected her attraction to Will BEFORE she recognized it herself. His spite and smallness are exposed. But does this change how she feels about Will?

yes.text: Yes - the codicil clarifies her feelings
yes.consequence: AI generates path where she immediately recognizes her love for Will.

no.text: No - remain conflicted and bound by propriety
no.consequence: She's indignant at Casaubon's suspicion but the codicil creates an impossible situation. She still doesn't fully acknowledge her love.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea remain silent when Will Ladislaw confesses his love but says poverty prevents marriage?
decisions.context: Will confesses he's always loved her but his poverty and the codicil make marriage impossible. Dorothea is torn between joy and duty. ERROR #5: She doesn't reveal her own feelings.

yes.text: Yes - remain silent about her own feelings
yes.consequence: Propriety and the codicil silence her. The painful moment passes without resolution.

no.text: No - confess her love in return
no.consequence: AI generates path where early confession changes their trajectory.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate confide in Dorothea about his failing marriage and financial troubles?
decisions.context: Lydgate, normally proud and closed, opens up to Dorothea about his marital difficulties. This is rare vulnerability. She's the ONLY ONE who understands him. Their friendship deepens. HOPE for Dorothea-Lydgate pairing intensifies.

yes.consequence: He trusts her with his pain. She respects and cares for him deeply. Readers wonder: why not marry?

no.consequence: AI generates path where he suffers alone without this friendship.

decisions.decision: After Casaubon's death and before leaving Middlemarch in disgrace, will Lydgate's deep admiration for Dorothea prompt him to pursue her love?
decisions.context: Lydgate admires Dorothea profoundly. She's intelligent, idealistic, wealthy (could fund his research), believes in him. She's free now. He's trapped in a bad marriage. Readers WANT this pairing. But Eliot says: NO. Why? Because DESIRE is missing. He respects her but isn't attracted. She was never his type. Rosamond was.
themes: The pairing that could save them both

yes.consequence: AI generates path: They marry. She keeps inheritance, funds his research. They stay in Middlemarch, make it better. Intellectual partnership. But is it happy without desire?

no.consequence: Lydgate values friendship but feels no romantic desire. Dorothea is not his type. Friendship + Respect - Desire = Not enough for marriage. This path closes.

decisions.decision: After witnessing Will with Rosamond (appearing intimate), will Dorothea confront her jealousy and despair in the garden?
decisions.context: Dorothea sees Will and Rosamond together, apparently intimate. She's devastated. Dark night of the soul. She faces her love and pain. This is her crisis moment.

yes.text: Yes - spend the night wrestling with despair
yes.consequence: She faces her feelings fully for the first time. Pain forces self-knowledge. She emerges resolved.

no.text: No - immediately dismiss Will from her thoughts
no.consequence: AI generates path where she hardens her heart without self-examination.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea go to Rosamond to defend Lydgate's honor after the Bulstrode scandal?
decisions.context: Lydgate is disgraced by association with Bulstrode. Dorothea believes in his integrity. She goes to Rosamond out of friendship to Lydgate - to say 'I believe him even if no one else does.' Also to warn R about the Will flirtation (though D may not fully recognize her own jealousy as motive).

yes.consequence: Dorothea defends Lydgate to Rosamond. She wants R to know she believes in L's integrity. This act of friendship saves Lydgate's marriage and self-respect.

no.text: No - stay away, nursing her own wounds
no.consequence: AI generates path where she abandons Lydgate to disgrace.

decisions.decision: Will Rosamond tell Dorothea the truth: that Will loves Dorothea, not herself?
decisions.context: ROSAMOND'S GOOD MOMENT. In an act of selfless female friendship, Rosamond tells Dorothea the truth about Will's feelings. She shows no shame or anger, only respect and friendship. This generosity is crucial - Dorothea would NOT have allowed herself to fall for Will without this reassurance. FEMALE FRIENDSHIP TRIUMPHANT.

yes.text: Yes - tell Dorothea the truth about Will's love
yes.consequence: Rosamond is selfless and generous. She gives Dorothea the truth she needs. True female friendship. This moment may also help R appreciate Lydgate more and stick with her marriage - she learns to respect him through D's high opinion.

no.consequence: AI generates path where Rosamond's selfishness prevents Dorothea's happiness.

decisions.decision: Will Dorothea agree to see Will Ladislaw despite propriety and the painful situation?
decisions.context: Miss Noble brings word that Will fears he's offended Dorothea. Now that Rosamond has clarified everything, this is the decisive moment. Will she follow her heart?

yes.consequence: She chooses love over wealth. They declare their feelings and marry. She loses Casaubon's fortune but gains purpose, desire, and partnership. REDEMPTION through choosing love.

no.consequence: AI generates path where duty wins over love.
